title: selliguea grammitoides
intro: Selliguea grammitoides, also known as the Maidenhair fern or Tawa fern, is a type of leafy green fern that belongs to the family Polypodiaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its various medicinal properties, including its ability to treat fever, cough, and respiratory issues, as well as its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of Selliguea grammitoides include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory function, and support the immune system, making it a popular remedy for various ailments. In traditional herbal preparations, the leaves and stems of the plant are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures that can be taken internally to harness its medicinal properties.
